The search results contain several recent papers addressing distribution shift in specific domains. Paper 2 is the most directly relevant to the core query as it explicitly tackles the fundamental pitfall of the i.i.d. assumption in deep learning and quantifies how real-world spatial distribution shifts cause substantial performance degradation in object detectors. By introducing a standardized benchmark (RWDS) for domain generalization across diverse environmental conditions, it perfectly supports the goal of contrasting controlled academic benchmarks with messy real-world deployment outcomes. I will select this paper and update the description to highlight its relevance to generalization failures, methodological limitations, and evaluation frameworks.
